     The Numbers Business Report

Hot Fuzz (2007)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $23,618,786Details
International Box Office $56,859,079
Worldwide Box Office $80,477,865
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $34,375,652
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $2,341,996
Total $36,717,648
Further financial details...

Synopsis

As a former London constable, Nicholas Angel finds if difficult to adapt to his new assignment in the sleepy British village of Sandford. Not only does he miss the excitement of the big city, but he also has a well-meaning oaf for a partner. However, when a series of grisly accidents rocks Sandford, Nick smells something rotten in the idyllic village.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$5,848,464 (24.8% of total gross)
Legs:4.04 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:29.3% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$16,000,000 (worldwide box office is 5.0 times production budget)
Theater counts:825 opening theaters/1,272 max. theaters, 5.5 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $41,058,237

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: April 20th, 2007 (Wide) by Focus Features
International Releases: February 16th, 2007 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
November 5th, 2020 (Wide) (Australia)
July 6th, 2023 (Wide) (New Zealand)
November 7th, 2024 (Limited) (New Zealand)
Video Release: July 31st, 2007 by Universal Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for violent content including some graphic images, and language.
Running Time: 121 minutes
Franchise: Three Flavours Cornetto Trilogy
Plot point: Buddy Cop, Police Detective, Serial Killer, Surprise Twist
Social setting: Life in a Small Town
Subgenre: Action Comedy
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Rogue Pictures, StudioCanal, Working Title Films, Big Talk Pictures
Production Countries: United Kingdom, France
Languages: English

Synopsis

As a former London constable, Nicholas Angel finds if difficult to adapt to his new assignment in the sleepy British village of Sandford. Not only does he miss the excitement of the big city, but he also has a well-meaning oaf for a partner. However, when a series of grisly accidents rocks Sandford, Nick smells something rotten in the idyllic village.
